working_tree: Benchmark executable and reports correspond to the implementation committed as 4d800f6ad87712f43595482626aa92c82ad44a06; evidence and final documentation were added afterward.
protocol: Each profile generated one DEFLATE level 6 source workbook in a separate process, ran two warm-ups, retained five measured editor-process runs and selected the elapsed-time median representative. Only targeted upsert was measured.
peak_memory_metric: Windows PROCESS_MEMORY_COUNTERS.PeakWorkingSetSize for each benchmark process; source fixture generation and Python verification are outside measured reused-source runs.
transform_metrics: Schema v6 records single-pass transform microseconds, parser/source-callback/coalesced/action traffic, output append/flush counts, peak owned output buffer, relationship scanner input calls/bytes/boundary carry/slow-path tags, relationship scan time, temporary-file write time and source-scan/action residual.
package_writer_metrics: Caller-owned internal telemetry enabled only for benchmark save records package and target worksheet entry open/read/write/close timing, bytes and call counts. Normal callers do not collect per-call clock data.
compatibility_protocol: The runner validated output-plan byte accounting and exact compressed payload equality for every raw-copy entry in every warm-up and measured output. Each median representative opened with openpyxl 3.1.2. The formula profile additionally streamed worksheet XML and inspected the worksheet relationship part to verify the A1 external hyperlink binding and target without loading a one-million-cell DOM.
performance_interpretation: The machine showed substantially slower and variable end-to-end timing than the prior same-machine bundle. Claims therefore use relationship scanner input/time, bounded buffers, process peak working set and compatibility evidence; they do not claim an overall throughput improvement.
scope: Single Windows/MSVC machine, numeric five-million-cell and formula/external-hyperlink one-million-cell sources, 1000 targeted upserts and output DEFLATE level 1. No arbitrary-workbook, random-editing, cross-machine or Office compatibility claim.
